preamble: Lowering of the open-mid front unrounded vowel [ɛ] to the open central vowel [a] is a common process in many dialects, often preceding further backing or as part of a push-shift.

examples.note: Lowering of [ɛ] to [a] in certain lexical items or phonological environments.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
